facts.text: China is one of the world's oldest continuous civilizations and one of its largest modern countries by population.
The Great Wall of China is a network of fortifications built and rebuilt across centuries rather than a single wall.
Beijing is China's capital, while Shanghai is its largest city by urban population.
The Yangtze River is the longest river in Asia.
Chinese history includes major dynasties such as the Han, Tang, Song, Yuan, Ming, and Qing.
Paper, gunpowder, the compass, and printing are often called the Four Great Inventions of ancient China.
The Terracotta Army near Xi'an was built to guard the tomb of Qin Shi Huang, China's first emperor.
Mandarin Chinese is the most widely spoken language in China, though many other languages and dialects are used across the country.
Chinese cuisine varies greatly by region, with famous culinary traditions from Sichuan, Cantonese, Hunan, Shandong, and elsewhere.
The Lunar New Year is one of the most important celebrations in Chinese culture.
China's geography ranges from deserts and steppes to rice paddies, river valleys, mountains, and dense megacities.
The giant panda became one of China's best-known national symbols and a global icon for wildlife conservation.
Modern China plays a major role in global manufacturing, technology, trade, infrastructure, and film markets.
The Forbidden City in Beijing served as the imperial palace for Ming and Qing emperors for nearly 500 years.
The Great Wall became one of the most famous landmarks associated with China.
Its scale helped make it one of the best-known structures in the world.
Great Wall of China is one of the standout topics people often explore within China.
Readers interested in China regularly come across Great Wall of China.
Learning about Great Wall of China helps explain part of the lasting appeal of China.
Great Wall of China remains one of the most recognizable examples connected with China.
Articles, documentaries, and fan discussions about China often highlight Great Wall of China.
For many people, Great Wall of China is a memorable entry point into China.
The story of Great Wall of China helps show why China stays so interesting to revisit.
Great Wall of China is frequently mentioned in guides and retrospectives about China.
One reason China feels rich and memorable is the presence of touchpoints like Great Wall of China.
Great Wall of China still stands out as a classic reference point within China.
